name: The Sound of Music
description: Rodgers and Hammerstein's 1965 musical film about Maria and the von Trapp family

questions.q: Who plays the governess Maria in the 1965 film The Sound of Music?

source.notes: Won Best Actress the previous year for Mary Poppins.

questions.q: Who plays Captain Georg von Trapp opposite Julie Andrews?

source.notes: Famously dismissed the film as 'The Sound of Mucus' for years.

questions.q: In what year was The Sound of Music released?

source.notes: Released March 2, 1965.

questions.q: In which Austrian city is the film primarily set and filmed?

source.notes: Boosted tourism to the city enormously.

questions.q: Which Rodgers and Hammerstein song teaches the children the musical scale?

source.notes: Filmed against a sweeping Salzburg travelogue.

questions.q: Which song was the final Oscar Hammerstein II lyric written before his death?

source.notes: Hammerstein died of cancer in August 1960.

questions.q: How many von Trapp children does Maria care for in the film?

source.notes: Liesl, Friedrich, Louisa, Kurt, Brigitta, Marta, and Gretl.

questions.q: Who directed the 1965 film The Sound of Music?

source.notes: Also directed West Side Story (1961); Wyler was the original director attached.

questions.q: Which historical event forces the von Trapp family to flee Austria in the film?

source.notes: Germany annexed Austria in March 1938.

questions.q: Who composed the music for The Sound of Music?

source.notes: Famous partnership with Hammerstein also produced Oklahoma! and The King and I.

questions.q: Who wrote the lyrics for the songs in The Sound of Music?

source.notes: This was his last completed musical.

questions.q: Which song does Maria teach the children to cheer them up during a thunderstorm?

source.notes: Later covered famously by John Coltrane.

questions.q: How many Academy Awards did The Sound of Music win, including Best Picture?

source.original_a: Three Academy Awards for its music
source.notes: Won five Oscars total; Best Picture, Director, Music, Sound, Editing.

questions.q: Which Hollywood actress was considered for Maria before Julie Andrews was cast?

source.notes: Hepburn reportedly turned down the role.

questions.q: What is the name of the eldest von Trapp daughter, famous for 'Sixteen Going on Seventeen'?

source.notes: Played by Charmian Carr.

questions.q: Which actor's singing voice was famously dubbed in the film?

source.notes: Dubbed by Bill Lee.

questions.q: Which abbess-sung anthem tells Maria to 'follow every rainbow'?

source.notes: Peggy Wood's vocal was dubbed by Margery MacKay.

questions.q: What is the name of the impresario pressuring the family to perform at the Salzburg Festival?

source.notes: Played by Richard Haydn.

questions.q: Which Austrian abbey serves as the wedding venue for Maria and Captain von Trapp in the film?

source.notes: The real Maria and Georg were married at Nonnberg in 1927.

questions.q: Who played the adoring Mother Abbess?

source.notes: Nominated for Best Supporting Actress.

questions.q: What is the first name of the Baroness whom Captain von Trapp is engaged to before falling for Maria?

source.notes: Played by Eleanor Parker.

questions.q: The 1965 film was based on a 1949 memoir by which real-life person?

source.notes: The Story of the Trapp Family Singers.
